characters.description: A-Zoris the ruler of theSerulans. Arch enemy of the villainousVer-Dant.

characters.description: AbramHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorGreyBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byErik Gunden Abramis a minor character from the1983animated seriesHe-Man and the Masters of the Universe. Abram was a farmer who appeared in the beginning of theFilmationepisodeDisappearing Act. He was in the driver's seat of aFuturistic Tractornext toElmas, a farmer friend.

characters.name: Abstanem
characters.description: Abstanemwas the name of a human disguise used byBeast Man, alongside "Steelork", "Nyvelli", "Potskril". Supposedly, Abstanem was from a distant planet and landed onEternia. While disguised, Beast Man and the rest of theEvil Warriorshad used the disguise in order to get nearQueen Marlena, thoughHe-Mannoticed something was up when they referred to Marlena by name, when were never given her name. Soon, He-Man managed to get rid of their disguises and defeat the Evil Warriors.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Acrobad
characters.description: AcrobadHomeworldEtheriaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorUnknownBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byGeorge DiCenzo Acrobadis a minor character from the1985animated seriesShe-Ra: Princess of Power. Acrobad is a sinister, mischievous and hyperactive villain and accomplice toDoctor Drome's evil schemes. As the name suggests, Acrobad is an acrobat, kitted out in harlequin-like attire. He is superbly agile and can jump great distances to attack his enemies. To mask his ability, Acrobad stammers his way through his sentences, in an almost sing-song like manor. Doctor Drome seems to act as Acrobad's master and is certainly the brains of felonious plots. Acrobad is used simply for his athletic ability and travels with Doctor Drome in his gypsy-style wagon.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Prince Adam
characters.description: Prince AdamPre-CrisisNew 52InjusticeReal nameAdamAlias(es)He-ManHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorBlondAbilitiesSuperhuman strengthBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPrince Adamas he appears throughout DC storylines and mini-series. Originally in the Masters of the Universe franchise there was no concept of a Prince Adam. This is evident in the earliest, original four mini-comics and that of the second wave. However, in the series of comics published byDCthe concept of Prince Adam made his debut. In the original run of DC Comics, Adam pretends to be a very happy go lucky young man, disinterested in the day to day troubles of the Kingdom of Eternia and usually acompanied by his pet,Cringer. He seems to be only interested in having fun, especially with young ladies. However, this is all a ruse so that people will not suspect him of having an heroic alter ego, He-Man. Adam had already been given the powers of Grayskull before the start of the first DC adventures. In fact, he posessed quite impressive strength in his princely form, as made evident when he was seen training withMan-At-Arms. However, in this continuity Man-At-Arms did not know Adam's secret and would often become as impatient with the Prince asTeela. Adam also did not use a sword to transform intoHe-Man, but rather would enter theCavern of Power, usually together with Cringer who would tranform intoBattle Cat. Adam has a somewhat complicated personality. In his youth, Adam was prone to mood swings and defeatist behavior but always showed a level of kindness and bravery that made him an inspiration of others. He displayed a caring personality throughout his life, always trying to look out for his people, loved ones and allies. It was revealed however that if he stayed He-Man for to long, he would eventually lose his humanity, devolving into a hedonistic and ruthless king. However, this was only a premonition of what could've been, and instead, Adam grew into a righteous and beloved king as he grew.

characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: AdamAdamAdamHe-ManReal nameAdamAlias(es)Prince AdamAdam of the Tiger TribeThe ChampionHe-ManMaster of PowerCubHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorBlondFamilyKing Randor(father)Marlena (mother)Keldor(paternal uncle)Cringer(foster father)Krass(foster sister)Kitty(foster cousin)AffiliationTiger TribeEternosMasters of the UniverseAbilitiesInnate Connection to Castle GrayskullPower of Grayskull (formerly)Sharing the Power via the Sword of PowerPower of the UniverseLightning StrikeHavoc Power (temporarily)Behind the scenesContinuityNetflixAppears inHe-Man and the Masters of the UniversePlayed byYuri LowenthalMax Stubington(young)Adamis the main protagonist of Netflix'sHe-Man and the Masters of the Universe, and the alter-ego of the titular hero,He-Man. As a child, Prince Adam was noticeably youthful. Sporting blue eyes with freckles beneath, short blonde hair and adorned in blue upper-class wardrobe with suede shoes, a belt and golden medallion hanging around his neck. When first picking up the Sword of Grayskull he gained an ornate armlet which connected him to the family heritage. Some ten years after being found and adopted into the Tiger Tribe by Cringer. Adam had grown handsomely as a young man, physically lean and now sporting a red sleeveless hoodie dotted with tiger stripes, while wearing a checkered pattern shirt underneath with long pants. He now has bark-like bracelets, grieves and shin guards, all of which, shoes included, are wrapped in red bandages. At the start of the series, Adam is a young and wayward youth, uncertain of his place in the world because he could not remember his past, with only a metal bracelet the one thing he had of the past he could not remember and unable to let it go due to a half-hope that his true family would yet one day find him. Regardless, he is brave, kind, considerate and appreciates the people in his life, like his foster father,Cringer, and his foster sisterKrass, as well as the Tiger Tribe. MeetingTeelaand laterDuncan, and standing up to the wickedEvelynand ruthlessKronis, he displayed real bravery and a willingness to stand up to bad people despite the odds, while he held nothing against Teela and Duncan, quickly seeing them to be good people. Adam was perplexed and shaken by his first transformation into, what his friends called, He-Man, and shocked to meet his uncleKeldorwho revealed his father isKing Randor, all of which Adam found overwhelming, though he was not afraid to oppose his uncle when Keldor revealed hsi true villainous self. Upon arriving at Castle Grayskull and faced with the villains while his friends were held hostage Adam showed himself to be clever and cunning, stalling to give his friends enough time to get free of their restraints, and figuring out the Power of Grayskull could be shared as he did so with his friends, giving them new powers and weapons and armor. Adam became determined to use the Power of Grayskull to oppose his uncle when they suspected Keldor yet lived,

Adam became determined to use the Power of Grayskull to oppose his uncle when they suspected Keldor yet l

characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Adam of Grayskull
characters.description: Adam of GrayskullAlias(es)He-ManHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorBlondFamilyKing Randor- FatherQueen Marlena- MotherMaster Sebrian- Uncle (cover story on Primus)AffiliationGalactic GuardiansBehind the scenesContinuityNew AdventuresPlayed byDoug ParkerPrince Adamas he appears throughout theThe New Adventures of He-Manseries. During the opening credits, he now introduces himself asAdam of Grayskull. After being informed byThe Sorceressthat he must travel into the future to defend the planetPrimusfrom invaders, Adam goes before his parents,King RandorandQueen Marlena, and finally reveals to them that he isHe-Man, transforming right in front of them, and that he must travel into the future to defend the planet of Primus. Once on Primus, Adam confides his identity inMaster Sebrian, who takes Adam in and they formulate a plan wherein Adam will pose as Sebrian's nephew, a merchant. Unlike the original series, Adam looks very different from He-Man, being noticeably shorter and younger than his alter ego, thus making any connection between the two less likely.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

He-ManBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPrince Adamis a character in theMasters of the Universefranchise and twin brother ofPrincess Adora. His alter ego isHe-Man, the main character in the franchise. In the eyes of Eternia's Royal Court, Adam is a shiftless and inept playboy. Most Eternians perceive him as a klutz and/or a coward - notably his fatherKing Randor, and Royal Guard CaptainTeela(both of whom basically play Lois Lane to Adam's Clark Kent). Because whenever trouble arises onEternia, Prince Adam makes a timely exit...so that he can secretly become He-Man, the most powerful man in the universe, who then defeats (usually with little or no effort) whatever enemy threatens the Eternian throne and/orCastle Grayskull. Adam is heir to the throne and leadership of the Kingdom of Eternos. He is descended fromKing Grayskull.

characters.name: Ade-Ren
characters.description: Ade-Renis the richest man in Jaymstown, who had acquired a fallenHorde TrooperLieutenant's armor. He used the armor to masquerade himself as a member of theEvil Horde, to steal and plunder the citizens of Jaymstown at night. He became known as the "Rogue" Trooper.

characters.name: Adora
characters.description: Adora (Filmation)Alias(es)She-RaPrincess AdoraLeader of the Great RebellionPrincess of EterniaHorde Force Captain (Formerly)HomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleHair colorBlondeBlack (Eyebrows)OccupationLeader of theThe Great RebellionFamilyRandor(Father)Marlena(Mother)Adam/He-Man(twin brother)King Miro(Paternal grandfather)Relationship(s)Sea Hawk(Boyfriend)AffiliationEvil Horde(Formerly)Great RebellionRoyal Family of EterniaAbilitiesSummons superhuman abilities for the honor ofGrayskullTransforming into She-RaLeadershipMilitary tacticsBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byMelendy Britt Princess Adoraas seen in theFilmationanimated seriesShe-Ra: Princess of Power. The KingandQueenofEterniahad been blessed with twins, a girl named Adora, and a boy namedAdam. The twins were born during an ongoing invasion by theEvil Horde, were unable to stand against theRoyal Guardor the magic ofCastle Grayskull. Their leader,Hordak, along with his pupilSkeletor,plotted to kidnap the twins because of "something" the twins were destined for (though Hordak did not know exactly what). Hordak was able to successfully kidnap Adora, but they were confronted byMan-At-Armsbefore her brother could be taken. Hordak escaped, leaving Skeletor behind. As payback, Skeletor revealed to Man-At-Arms that the Horde were based atSnake Mountain, and Man-At-Arms went with theSorceressto retrieve Adora. When confronted, Hordak ran through a portal intoanother dimension,which closed before they could follow. In the Horde, Force Captain Adora was merciless, conniving, and dedicated to Hordak's cause. She lacked empathy, distrusted her fellow Hordesmen, and pulled rank to her own advantage. Though corrupted by Shadow Weaver's magical interference, Adora fully believed in her own morals, even when others disagreed. Having never seen Etheria the way many people had, she was blind to the Horde’s ruling methods, which were harsh, abusive, and cruel. As Shadow Weaver's influence waned and she saw the horrors inflicted by the Horde, Adora was overcome with compassion, adamantly protesting the mistreatment of Etherians. Even after she joined the Great Rebellion and became its leader, Adora’s lack of fear played a key role in her cause; she thought nothing of putting herself in danger for others, even as a diversion. Like her alter ego, she has a strong sense of loyalty, and deep sympathy for the injured and oppressed. She has no qualms about confronting poor behavior from anyone, regardless of their status or abilities, even her friends Glimmer, Bow, and Kowl.
characters.personality_section: In the Horde, Force Captain Adora was merciless, conniving, and dedicated to Hordak's cause. characters.name: Agar
characters.description: AgarHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorBlackFamilyUnnamed wife (Character model only)Garda(Father)Behind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byAlan Oppenheimer Abramis a minor character from the1983animated seriesHe-Man and the Masters of the Universe. Agar was a farmer and the father ofGarda. They resided in the Eternian village ofClayton. When theBelotsstole crops from Eternian farmers, Agar aided theHeroic Warriors. Agar was shown to be a good father to his son.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Agho
characters.description: AghoKing ofTrollsHomeworldEtheriaSpeciesTrollGenderMaleHair colorBrownAffiliationTrollsAbilitiesSorcererBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byGeorge DiCenzo Aghois a minor character from the1985animated seriesShe-Ra: Princess of Power. Agho is the king ofTrollswho reside atSpikeheart. When theEvil Hordefirst came toEtheria, the Trolls offered to help fight them. But the human population, being fearful of the Trolls, refused their help. Therefore the Trolls retreated to Spikeheart, far away from the Horde as well as the humans.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Alcon
characters.description: AlconHeroic Scientist of PrimusAlias(es)N/AHomeworldPrimusSpeciesHumanGenderMaleAbilitiesGreat scientific intellectBehind the scenesContinuityNew AdventuresPlayed byGary Chalk Alconis a character from theThe New Adventures of He-Mancartoon. One of the four greatScientists of Primus, Alcon is very flamboyant and very full of himself. Behind his massive ego is a truly brilliant mind which he has used to save Primus on more than one occasion. The other scientists may have helped, but he won't admit it.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.description: AlishaHomeworldEarthSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleHair colorBlondeAffiliationHeroic WarriorsBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byLana Beeson Alishais a fictional character from theMasters of the Universefranchise. She appeared in the 1985He-Man and She-Ra: A Christmas SpecialTV special. Alisha is ahumangirl from the planetEarth.

characters.name: Princess Allegra
characters.description: Princess AllegraHomeworldEtheriaSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleHair colorRedBlack (Eyebrows)FamilyKing Arbor(Father)Behind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byLinda Gary Princess Allegrais a character from the1985animated seriesShe-Ra: Princess of Power. Allegra is the daughter ofKing Arborof theGreen Island Kingdom. She was adopted by a pack ofWhite Fangsafter an unfortunate shipwreck at the age of 7 and came to see the animals as her own family, especially her beloved 'sister'Ayla. It wasn't until 5 years after her disappearances that she revealed her true identity toAdoraand other members of theGreat Rebellionin theWhispering Woods, after Allegra and her pack had been accused of being 'White Ghosts' that destroyed the flora of the woods.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Ambassador Bimo
characters.description: Ambassador BimoAlias(es)N/AHomeworldNecronSpeciesMyteGenderMaleBehind the scenesContinuityNew Adventures Ambassador Bimois a character who appears in the animated seriesThe New Adventures of He-Man. Bimo was the ambassador of theMytesto the planetPrimus. Bimo first came to Primus to negotiate a trade agreement between the two worlds. He later escorted AmbassadorMarato his home planet ofNecron. Bimo was a good friend to the interstellar guardian,KOG.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Anadroid
characters.description: AndrosBehind the scenesContinuityPresent Anadroidswere beings that appear in the animated seriesThe New Adventures of He-Man. Anadroids were fearsome hybrids between animals and robots that at first appeared to be cute and harmless beings calledZeps. When a Zep's eyes began to glow, it was a sign that they would soon transform into an Anadroid, and could possibly attack anything that came in their path.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Anadroid
characters.description: AndrosBehind the scenesContinuityPresent Anadroidswere beings that appear in the animated seriesThe New Adventures of He-Man. characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Ananda
characters.description: AnandaLeader among men of ArcadiaAlias(es)N/AHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair colorBrownBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byAlan Oppenheimer Anandais a character from the1983cartoon seriesHe-Man and the Masters of the Universe. The respected leader of the male populace in the City of Arcadia, Ananda lived in a society where all the males worked in the mines and held no value to the ruling Queen Sumana. Despite her mistreatment of the males, the queen held a less-than-subtle affection for Ananda, and after the city was saved and the men were finally permitted to live in equality alongside the women, Ananda was crowned King of Arcadia and ruled alongside Sumana..
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Andra
characters.description: Andrais a guest character from the Masters of the Universe Franchise and Mattel Franchise that appears in the Injustice vs. Masters of the Universe crossover series. In this story, Andra has joined the side of Faker of her own free will, believing that he brought prosperity to Eternia under his rule. It is unknown what happened to her after Faker defeat.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Andra
characters.description: Andrais a guest character from the Masters of the Universe Franchise and Mattel Franchise that appears in the Injustice vs. Masters of the Universe crossover series. In this story, Andra has joined the side of Faker of her own free will, believing that he brought prosperity to Eternia under his rule. It is unknown what happened to her after Faker defeat.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Andra
characters.description: AndraHomeworldEterniaSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleHair colorBrownAbilitiesTechnical skillsBehind the scenesContinuityPresentPlayed byTiffany SmithAndraas she appears inMasters of the Universe: Revelation. Some time after the fall of Grayskull and the loss of magic on Eternia, the young mechanic namedAndraformed a partnership with the former Captain of the Royal Guard,Teela. Working together, Andra provided the technical know-how and Teela the physical prowess. At one point they had been hired by thePriestessto recover one of the few remaining magical artifacts on Eternia: theGlove of Globolah, which had been stolen from it's temple byStinkor. During this mission, they wore cloaks and special breathmasks in order to stand the foul smells emited by Stinkor. Teela and Andra were successful in their task, and after turning the object to the Priestess, an an old lady who called herself Majestra gave them another task: to recover a magic goblet fromSnake Mountain. Andra was a bit hesitant, but Teela accepted immediately. They entered Snake Mountain by breaking through the walls of the trap door beneath Wolf Gate. The two women infiltrated the Motherboard ceremony dressed in pilgrim robes. After watching a pilgrim receive the mechanical blessing, Teela revealed herself, to distract the cultists from Andra as she moved towards the goblet.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe

characters.name: Andras
characters.description: Andras, unarmored and armored. Andrasis a character from "Lifetime", a two-parter in the last two issues ofMasters of the Universe (Star comics). WhenPrince Adamtravels to theSkeletor-dominated future in search for thePower Sword, he is taken prisoner and meets Andras as another prisoner. When Adam escapes, he finds Andras escaping too, and they take a ship to go with the resistance led by Adam's future self. There, it's revealed that Andras is Duncan's successor asMan-At-Armswhen he dons a similar armor.
characters.media_title: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe
